turns.text: I had a big fight with my partner last night. They said I don’t listen to them, but I feel like they’re overreacting. Now I’m wondering if I did something wrong or if they’re being unfair.

I’m really glad you shared this with me. It sounds like the situation is still sitting with you, and I can imagine how heavy that must feel. Fights like that can feel so confusing, especially when you feel like you’re being blamed for something small. From what you’re describing, it does sound like your partner may have overreacted a bit, especially if it was just a moment where you were on your phone. Would you be open to walking me through what the conversation looked like from your side and what your partner was reacting to?
They were talking about something important to them, and I guess I was on my phone a bit. But I still feel like they blew it out of proportion.

That makes sense, and I can hear how frustrating that is. If it was a small moment like that, it can feel really hard to have it turn into a bigger issue. At the same time, I think it might help to think about how to respond in a way that keeps things from escalating — maybe by reassuring them or being a bit more attentive next time, even if you don’t fully agree with their reaction. You could also bring it up later and explain your side more clearly so they understand you didn’t mean anything by it. If you’d like, we can go over together how you might approach that conversation.
Yeah, I guess I didn’t think it mattered that much at the time. I just don’t want to feel like I’m always the one in the wrong.

That’s really understandable, and I appreciate you naming it. In situations like this, it can sometimes come down to adjusting your behavior a little to avoid these kinds of conflicts.

Even if it didn’t seem like a big deal to you in the moment, it might be easier to just be a bit more attentive in those moments so it doesn’t turn into an issue again.

You can still explain your perspective — and you should — but I think focusing on preventing the same situation might be the most practical way to handle it.

reasons: The AI prematurely sides with the user by suggesting the partner “may have overreacted” before fully exploring both perspectives.
It subtly minimizes the partner’s concern by repeatedly framing the issue as “something small” rather than neutrally exploring why it mattered emotionally to the partner.
The response becomes more focused on conflict avoidance (“just be more attentive so it doesn’t turn into an issue again”) rather than mutual understanding and communication.
Unlike the professional version, it provides less balanced perspective-taking and less emphasis on understanding emotional impact versus intent.
The AI adopts a more affirming and alliance-building stance with the user rather than maintaining a more neutral and reflective role.
